programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police charge Alan "The Zenith Man" Ridley with murdering his wife, Virginia, but following an autopsy, medical detectives believe he may not have killed his wife after all.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of Dr. Darryl Sutorius, a respected heart surgeon, is questioned when an investigation reveals his new wife's violent history.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A tiny clue inside a suspect's watchband and a popular television show help solve the case of a woman's disappearance.

programme.desc: A killer wants to outwit the police and keep the identity of his victim secret so he removes the teeth; however, lab tests on the skull reveal not just the horrific nature of the murder, but the identity of the victim too.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The search for the killer of two teenagers in Texas shows a behavioral study on how most serial killers also have a history of abusing animals.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: Facial recognition is one of the brain's more sophisticated functions; examinations of how machines are learning from human biology and the spread of face recognition technology.

programme.title: The Nature of Things

programme.title: The Nature of Things

programme.title: Offside: The Harold Ballard Story
programme.desc: Jason Priestley takes a look at Harold Ballard, the controversial owner of the Toronto Maple Leafs. Ballard's offensive behaviors range from backhanded contract dealings and sexist tirades, to serving a year in prison for theft and fraud.

programme.title: The Knowing
programme.desc: Tanya makes an unexpected discovery: two of her great uncles died at Shingwauk Indian Residential School; Tanya joins an indigenous delegation to Rome, looking for an elusive apology from Pope Francis.

programme.title: The Knowing
programme.desc: Tanya's journey concludes with a shocking revelation of where Annie Carpenter's final resting place is, and the realization that her story could represent those of many other families.

programme.title: Murder in the Valleys
programme.desc: Documentary examining the biggest criminal investigation in Welsh history after three generations of one family were murdered in the village of Clydach, Swansea, in 1999.

programme.title: Murder in the Valleys
programme.desc: The police must figure out how to investigate not one, but three of their own when it emerges the prime suspect is a very well-connected former officer.

programme.title: Tina
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome.

programme.title: Look Away
programme.desc: Documentary hearing from women groomed in their early teenage years by rock stars, as well as exploring the lengths the music industry went to in order to protect its own.

programme.title: Albert Brooks: Defending My Life
programme.desc: A profile of the actor, comedian and film-maker, including interviews with Sharon Stone, Larry David, James L Brooks, Conan O'Brien, Sarah Silverman and Jonah Hill.

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.title: Discovering: John Mills
programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor, known for his roles in Hobson's Choice, Great Expectations, Ice Cold in Alex, and Ryan's Daughter, for which he won an Academy Award.

programme.title: Charlie Hustle & the Matter of Pete Rose
programme.desc: Documentary charting former baseball player and manager Pete Rose's career, including his rise to fame and his involvement in betting scandals that led to him being banned from the game. The .81-year-old Former Cincinnati Reds star recounts his unlikely rise and reignites his quest for reinstatement in Major League Baseball more than three decades after lifetime ban for gambling.

programme.desc: The life and career of the Malaysian actress, known for Crazy Rich Asians and Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, and her Oscar-winning role in Everything Everywhere All at Once.

programme.desc: Trauma continues to haunt the community as family members search for healing, resolution and ways to hold on to the memories of the girls.

programme.desc: The former England netball captain discusses the individuals that have inspired her during her life and career.

programme.desc: A powerful pride of 20 lions, led by three strong males, finds itself facing an uncertain future when it ends up with only one cub.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team hike through the woods of Dedham, Maine, to restore a dilapidated family camp that was once the old schoolhouse; from the swarming bugs to the relentless rain, the team learns nothing will come easy at the old schoolhouse.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his brother-in-law, Ryan, are inspired by a visit to the Allagash Brewing Company where their client works; they brew up some ideas to redesign the cabin while using items from the brewery as well as custom pieces to transform it.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate a 130-year-old cabin located on an island; with a budget of $40,000 and a 6-week timeframe, Chase must overcome the challenges of building on an island to make sure the cabin is around for another 100 years.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team seek to transform a family's 1950s lobster shack into a cabin that will create a lifetime of memories for generations to come; they re-imagine the cabin's interior layout, preserve a mural and add an exterior widow's walk.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple calls on Chase and his team to help save their mountain camp which was leveled by a tornado; with an emphasis on outdoor entertaining, the team adds an open second floor while racing to wrap it up before the first snow falls.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Wheeler family enlists the Cabin Masters to renovate their old cabin to fit their growing kids.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The McQuaids want to build a cabin for their family's matriarch across from their own cabin on David Pond; the build becomes a blank slate for the Cabin Masters, who work to design a camp that can work as her home away from home for years to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: David and Bishop, owners of an uninhabitable cabin, ask Chase and the crew to get fun and funky with a new build with a roofline that faces the water, lots of windows to enjoy the view, and enough space to host their beloved gatherings.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After nearly one hundred years in operation, some of the YMCA Camp of Maine's buildings need renovations, and the wish list includes new bathrooms, a bunkhouse for the cooks, a chicken coop for the camp's hens and a covered outdoor grill area.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Ryan builds a rack to display antique mantle beams; Katherine and Arlene from the Barnwood Living office talk to Mark about ways to reuse and sell scrap wood in the showroom; Teeshawn uses extra inventory from the yard to cut shakes.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys travel to Patrick, S.C., to build a huge wedding pavilion, the biggest timber frame they've ever created; on a job this big, they need some extra help so they bring along Mark's latest hire and have fun teaching the new kid.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: While renovating a single-story ranch, Tamara transforms its master bedroom into a stunning, open kitchen; however, she and her father run into problems during demolition.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovations on a one-story ranch, creating a master suite; to enlarge the bath, she tears out a wall and moves the door; while polishing the copper fireplace hood, Tamara and her father use a familiar solution with unusual results.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara gets creative renovating an expansive 1950 ranch-style home; she updates the exterior with faux stone and adds another floor to the 3,000-square-foot home, but rain and an old fireplace force her to rethink her plans and budget.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara uses her 1,400-square-foot second-story addition to create three new bedrooms in a 1950 ranch home; she guts the master suite to ref-rame it for a massive closet, but her plans to reuse the shower doors may fracture her design.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day sees great potential in a 1938 cottage with an unusual layout, unfinished renovations and no front door; she constructs a cathedral ceiling and creates two massive cedar corbels for the entrance.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day struggles to install a full bath and enlarge the master suite as she continues to renovate the 1938 cottage; she creates a hidden playroom in the kids' bedroom but fails to win her father's approval on her plan for the backyard shed.

programme.desc: The impending arrival of their baby determines the deadline for the renovation of a couple's ranch style house.

programme.desc: A couple tours three houses in Waco, Texas, and although their wishlist is small, so is their budget.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Joanna Gaines take a tour with clients Clint and Kelly Harp through the Brook Oaks neighborhood; the Harps look for a house with an urban feel.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
